//: Reduced-prompt profile for CI-failure investigation. Launch with: claude --settings .claude/settings.investigate.json. The deny list (Edit/Write/NotebookEdit, mutating git/gh, rm/mv) wins over allow and blocks those actions. IMPORTANT: the allow list below is a convenience to cut prompts on common read-only commands; it is NOT a sandbox and must NOT be treated as the security boundary. Prefix allows cannot constrain composed shell syntax, so an allowed command can still write or exfiltrate in ways the rules do not model -- e.g. 'grep x f > f' overwrites via output redirection, and the node/python3 helper allows execute repo-local .claude/tools/* scripts whose child processes (file/network access) are unconstrained. The REAL boundary for an untrusted PR (external, unreviewed, or one that may have modified .claude/* or anything the skill runs) is the ENVIRONMENT: run inside a throwaway git worktree, on a trusted base-branch checkout of .claude (tools, hooks, and these settings), with no credentials. Two commands are intentionally NOT allow-listed because a wildcard cannot constrain them: the play.clickhouse.com query (the PreToolUse hook allow_play_query.py auto-approves only the exact read-only SELECT-to-play shape; any other curl prompts) and archive extraction (tar -x is prompt-gated -- an appended -C / or crafted archive could overwrite files; only read-only tar -tzf listing is allowed).
